member.name: T:Mnemonic
member.summary: BIP-39 24-word mnemonic phrases compatible with the Android and iOS mobile wallets.

member.name: M:Mnemonic.#ctor(System.String[])
member.summary: The list of words in this mnemonic.

member.name: M:Mnemonic.Generate12
member.summary: Returns a new random 12-word mnemonic from the BIP-39 standard English word list.

member.name: M:Mnemonic.Generate24
member.summary: Returns a new random 24-word mnemonic from the BIP-39 standard English word list.

member.name: M:Mnemonic.FromString(System.String)
member.summary: Recover a mnemonic from a string, splitting on spaces.
member.param: The string to recover the mnemonic from
member.exception: if the mnemonic does not pass validation.

member.name: M:Mnemonic.FromWords(System.Collections.Generic.IList{System.String})
member.summary: Construct a mnemonic from a 24-word list. {@link Mnemonic#validate()} is called before returning, and it will throw an exception if it does not pass validation. An invalid mnemonic can still create valid Ed25519 private keys, so the exception will contain the mnemonic in case the user wants to ignore the outcome of the validation.
member.param: the 24-word list that constitutes a mnemonic phrase.
member.exception: if the mnemonic does not pass validation.
member.remarks: @see#validate() the function that validates the mnemonic.

member.name: M:Mnemonic.ToStandardECDSAsecp256k1PrivateKeyImpl(System.String,System.Int32[])
member.param: the passphrase used to protect the mnemonic, use "" for none
derivation path as an integer array, see: `calculateDerivationPathValues`

member.name: M:Mnemonic.ToPrivateKey
member.returns: the recovered key; use {@link PrivateKey#derive(int)} to get a key for an account index (0 for default account)

member.name: M:Mnemonic.ToPrivateKey(System.String)
member.summary:
the passphrase used to protect the mnemonic the recovered key; use {@link PrivateKey#derive(int)} to get a key for an account index (0 for default account) @deprecateduse {@link #toStandardEd25519PrivateKey(String, int)} ()} or {@link #toStandardECDSAsecp256k1PrivateKey(String, int)} (String, int)} instead Recover a private key from this mnemonic phrase.

This is not compatible with the phrases generated by the Android and iOS wallets; use the no-passphrase version instead. @seePrivateKey#fromMnemonic(Mnemonic, String)

member.name: M:Mnemonic.ToLegacyPrivateKey
member.exception: when there are issues with the mnemonic

member.name: M:Mnemonic.ToSeed(System.String)
member.summary: Convert passphrase to a byte array.

member.name: M:Mnemonic.CalculateDerivationPathValues(System.String)
member.summary: Converts a derivation path from string to an array of integers. Note that this expects precisely 5 components in the derivation path, as per BIP-44: `m / purpose' / coin_type' / account' / change / address_index` Takes into account `'` for hardening as per BIP-32, and does not prescribe which components should be hardened.
member.param: the derivation path in BIP-44 format, e.g. "m/44'/60'/0'/0/0"
member.returns: an array of integers designed to be used with PrivateKey#derive

member.name: M:Mnemonic.ToStandardEd25519PrivateKey(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Recover an Ed25519 private key from this mnemonic phrase, with an optional passphrase.
member.param: the passphrase used to protect the mnemonic

member.name: M:Mnemonic.ToStandardECDSAsecp256k1PrivateKey(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Recover an ECDSAsecp256k1 private key from this mnemonic phrase, with an optional passphrase. Uses the default derivation path of `m/44'/3030'/0'/0/${index}`.
member.param: the passphrase used to protect the mnemonic, use "" for none

member.name: M:Mnemonic.ToStandardECDSAsecp256k1PrivateKeyCustomDerivationPath(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Recover an ECDSAsecp256k1 private key from this mnemonic phrase and derivation path, with an optional passphrase.
member.param: the passphrase used to protect the mnemonic, use "" for none
the derivation path in BIP-44 format, e.g. "m/44'/60'/0'/0/0"
